summary: Create low-fidelity COMPOSITE terrain patches, barriers/channels, strategic nodes, route corridors, and governance overlays
acceptance_criteria: Create low-fidelity COMPOSITE strategic terrain fixture content using distinct TerrainPatch, BarrierChannel, StrategicNode, RouteCorridor, and DistrictGovernanceFootprint overlay records.

Do not present exact historical borders, port importance, river importance, pass claims, road claims, polity labels, cultural labels, or language labels as fact unless historical_researcher records source review with HISTORICAL/INFERRED/COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL tagging.
Starting assumptions only: 12-18 governance footprints, 18-30 strategic nodes, 25-45 route corridors, 12-24 terrain patches or bands, and 6-14 barriers/channels.

The fixture must show valleys or terrain bands, at least one barrier-without-crossing, at least one pass or crossing bottleneck, at least one river/coastal corridor, at least one road-width/capacity bottleneck, one visual-neighbor-without-route case, and one visually-distant-explicit-route case.
Governance footprints must visibly overlap or group terrain and route features without becoming route authority or hit-test priority.

Every advantage has a cost/dependency/counter in explicit local data or reason text: for example pass control requires the pass node/corridor, river movement depends on channel/port/season, and wide roads expose capacity targets.

Content validation rejects missing endpoints, duplicate terrain/node/corridor ids, route nodes outside valid strategic placement rules, barrier crossings without explicit nodes/corridors, governance adjacency used as reachability, hidden grid/lattice/sequential-id shortcuts, and disconnected playable graphs without explicit reason codes.
required_tests: focused content compiler tests for terrain patch, barrier/channel, strategic node, route corridor, and governance overlay separation
negative fixture tests for historical overclaim, barrier-without-crossing, governance-as-route shortcut, hidden grid/lattice leakage, duplicate ids, and missing endpoint rejection
notes: This task must route historical details to historical_researcher before formal content claims are accepted.
Low-fidelity COMPOSITE data is allowed; high-fidelity final art and formal GIS historical data are not.
No forced migration, labor extraction, famine, enslavement, massacre, or religious conflict may be represented as a cost-free terrain or route reward.
next_action: Start fixture/content work after M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 closed in main.
